title: astragalus missouriensis var. accumbens
intro: Astragalus missouriensis var. accumbens, also known as Missouri milkvetch, is a perennial legume plant that belongs to the pea family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing stress, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Astragalus missouriensis var. accumbens can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or powder, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
